textByHash.1e5211bbc85e752ca827c51c3408bcadd9029c9a: That fool Sigrid! What does she think she's doing?
textByHash.1730ae8ca9512aa8cca68c1bd7055391509ca9ac: Ah. I apologise, but I'm rather busy at the moment.
textByHash.ffa135701d295157b07290be8437526f8b53eaac: I'm busy. You'd better talk to Ghrim.
textByHash.8683862e29a726657f125a52872b575c31edd0a5: I'm sorry, , but I'm very busy. Talk to my son and daughter if Ghrim can't help you.
textByHash.db0fcea687fd22dca427cbd21997f757c482be97: What is it? You'll have to excuse me, I'm quite busy...
textByHash.5dee98dcf76e976493ad53b05cbce8d9f909ea39: Affairs of state, and so on.
textByHash.322af70dda6ec66ca537a87145ceb719c803e7cc: Well, there's not all that much for me to do these days, you see.
textByHash.c65b433230801517d9bce6e21297c18ad762435d: And though you do make a fine regent, you cannot be here all the time.
textByHash.68127a8e2d589619d61e581277c6d2c8048e4256: Well, yes. Of course, Sigrid was the one who insulted me first.
textByHash.a122d4628de3c25b7abd53048ce3aa95a78d1430: And that's the least of the kingdom's problems at the moment.
textByHash.8a78116803894b4937ee819025b65f571447f39a: Some of Frodi's barrels are missing, as are many of Magnus's crates. Leif also says he's had stacks of wood disappear.
textByHash.29b309ffd303c19a5faca48e88e1630d3cdd1094: Since you're the regent, you should investigate what has happened to these missing resources.
textByHash.13c0f4009a042f63b3b0bf086bef529bad8ad3ff: I'm glad to see the kingdom in such capable hands.
textByHash.f2a7c861d198fee67425c2d6fa8a297a16fbe2f1: That never prevented me from ruling this kingdom when I was your age.
textByHash.b2cd97515185ee5e576f798bad1479ce2fea4145: I'm glad to have your help.
textByHash.74a9bdd48d5ff12d2562ea94677c696551ffc97f: All you need to do for the moment is to talk to the citizens of Miscellania, and find out what has been happening.
textByHash.e84d16ad31959d475182157698b0ecf282df2172: It might also be useful to interrogate the citizens of Etceteria to see what they know.
textByHash.0a99e9e4b204bb14c93ed7648ef803e98977ad00: Have you spoken to the citizens yet?
textByHash.a7a12dc3795e51e30fb101c6e3230a0084290c89: So, Sigrid's planning to invade, is she?
textByHash.6523d905efbc644908054f668bbb1378f549a812: Or perhaps she's claiming that Miscellania is part of Etceteria now!
textByHash.c64ead69ca881f53a5b3339aa8fca6bd8dbe9237: , go and talk to Sigrid and ask her what she thinks she's doing.
textByHash.817ff82dcb94516dbad33cbe01d413e59f3ac9c2: Exchanging angry letters is understandable, but where did she get an army from?
textByHash.330e66bc8428e38b21e825542fff3c52977a3bd0: I see. Sigrid thinks that now I've recognised Etceteria as a sovereign nation, I'll just roll over and play dead when she tries to invade, does she?
textByHash.43b5d11c43daa077635ef6ad4a76ec6eef8642ba: And she's even started spreading rumours among her own citizens! Are there no depths to which she won't sink?
textByHash.b9889da4f9cd6b75611e976dc408ec4774b37377: Lying to her own people about there being Miscellanian soldiers... we don't even have an army!
textByHash.3cdf3e5d57e9757fde17382bc439acbbe213a977: Which makes it all the worse, as otherwise I'd be able to invade Etceteria to save the citizens from Sigrid's depravity.
textByHash.e8072d497ee137ec6ced1e1958392354e9b0db7f: In any case, if there are any 'Miscellanian soldiers', they must have come from somewhere.
textByHash.b4259c286eda82739f5c4ca2af0669142784fc37: Ask Advisor Ghrim if he knows who they might be, or where they might have come from.
textByHash.901a05bfafc1dc15a6a6dd798e3157babc071641: He's a capable advisor, so I'm sure he'll know about any suspicious people.
textByHash.5d9b248e0ebe3ed422d30554e320ea883e4f2e40: Of course, how could I have forgotten?
textByHash.165ebe86ea64527c998fdb6a5449a1535662a222: I'm surprised I didn't tell you when you were appointed Regent.
textByHash.fcf9760f4174d99c481a59b03303d0afbbac0d37: Ah, so you wish to look in the dungeons in case these new arrivals are to blame?
textByHash.35a508b899623202d62f597b1692b8e3eef173bc: I doubt that anyone with Miscellanian relatives would be at fault. However...
textByHash.41b04285081375ac533702aa2a830d00e23f7918: There is something I need you to do in the dungeons.
textByHash.dd6f3edc429758cef2bac44d813cde682a2701bc: They are still being excavated, and the dwarven architects are finding new caves as they go. There have been reports of odd sightings in these new caves.
textByHash.705f6c815c99739d5153e044d87de4c06efa5028: Perhaps they are monsters, waiting to devour unwary Miscellanians...
textByHash.2b0a9db22943eadc7c23cfbd6d8deee2a57ef851: Whatever they are, I want you to look into it.
textByHash.971272eded08f2e6051feaf529b1d36cd54fd708: You are a member of the royal family, after all, so I know I can rely on your help.
textByHash.a56fc03a58e59110b020edbcbb7281aa220dfc5d: The guard by the dungeon entrance may not recognise you, since he was only hired recently. Here, take this scroll to show that you have access to the dungeons.
textByHash.b01114a51fd9b4414d6777d09b64a649e84c54bb: You'll find the entrance by the south-east corner of the palace.
textByHash.babc1f3c8646902c9a36386ed35916d243fec346: Have you given that scroll to the guard yet?
textByHash.c78a1b88fe31d103f628ea62a19b9e08a64bada0: Honestly... very well, I'll write you another one.
textByHash.64431377c20ea1a45adfe3040ed09271f7b78626: Try not to lose it this time.
textByHash.4e3d1729670c7feab31f26de7f20389f1847668c: Why didn't they just go through the trials as usual?
textByHash.818473600c48a981d15a9afbece750f438e854e8: Unless they're the ones that the Council warned me about.
textByHash.cb95468b87b18b8ce628104dedc8fe3b40dfe05d: Still, I can't believe they'd start a war between Miscellania and Etceteria, just to show they were adults...
textByHash.3bac656d88457ba5f37dcb70a58f4a3f3c232d36: But at least you've found the missing goods. Do you have them with you?
textByHash.30b6020ce602f758df08d0c0de16c98bcd6b325e: We'll have to send people down to collect them later, then.
textByHash.a657f68e89991e9aa72130722e40776abb64804d: Oh? Do you have this letter with you?
textByHash.9b957509ac30758376272b9bff6b933a0167cc8b: You'd better ask Sigrid to write you another one, then.
textByHash.5fa68a159819bb59cbb4ffdc70ee34144ff9bd2d: I'd like to see what she has to say.
textByHash.58373385e1a6183991d2f792d064542f75eef0fd: You defeated that sea snake? Sigrid seems rather impressed with that.
textByHash.7397302ef4d7f368baa9104e9ac2aa0ea22f5c9c: And the caverns underneath her palace are joined to ours, I see.
textByHash.f8a094f46111c6c42838437fdcd7d7daa03b4c70: She thinks we should join forces to develop them for more houses.
textByHash.e312a148f51e701180e7a75ce4d0cfa28892afab: Finally... hmm. That's not what I'd have expected from Sigrid.
textByHash.50c48f072a2b3d9f8b1e33205f196a1d6e87812a: She, erm, says if I weren't a yeti, she might want to get to know me better.
textByHash.d01fd7c953dd440404e37e6c3859e8f6f822ed9b: Since we're both warmongering fools, she thinks we'd get on well.
textByHash.73375bbc025b2bc5387fa806342fda7babc4c475: I suppose she has a point there.
textByHash.6beb06dec5e48c2c25401166487cb3f6eba3ca83: No, no. I'll think about it for a while.
textByHash.adc0e5857850bc2eb548c2d3ee97dfe5aef0f7fd: Though I'd like to tell her I'm sorry for calling her a warmongering harpy.
textByHash.611019eb5e85df5be8172ea0a3b9fc1bd83eab11: In the meantime, , you have managed to avert a war and bring the culprits to light.
textByHash.a7686d95af896c2a6e6bdcb803f4429b148f0d28: The kingdom of Miscellania will have to reward you for this.
textByHash.6bc1388e0465c2667320ee24c1afd6da6a8fad05: I hear that the Etceterians are also grateful for your help, and have pledged some of their resources.
textByHash.5d8ecea3cc88ec905bc968a2e0e2069b801163de: You can talk to Ghrim to deal with those, just as before.
textByHash.0079a27828c42714dee59e74d84e31aabab5f0d9: Thank you for your work in bringing peace back to these islands.
textByHash.4d664c85f04d887f5e5e0da9716064157988f60e: I hear that the Etceterians have also pledged to give you some of their resources, as thanks for preventing war once again.
textByHash.2a1e8b9abfc0039ac9d81a8411ee54d38639ac4d: Remember, speak to Ghrim if you require assistance.
textByHash.a0f3ebcc909a824b6cd2743e1736b981465c532e: Who are you? Who seeks audience with the King of this fair land of Miscellania?
textByHash.491dc5b802a256b2f29781735826ddb14de8edfe: Ah, it pleases me to see such enthusiasm!
textByHash.c6970c6e382d79627750ff41fa8c8f299045b6ba: I was once an adventurer such as yourself; even while fulfilling my duties as King of Miscellania, I would roam the lands in search of adventure, and strike down mighty foes with a single blow!
textByHash.3929e00806133d872271e7db825312e425cba7c7: Unfortunately, I was a little too daring, and attempted to brave the icelands, where I finally met my match.
textByHash.bb0f0b004b838262b70393299064b31e3e427dea: I survived, barely, but I was cursed for the rest of my life to stay in this form. Which leaves me incapable of performing my duties as ruler.
textByHash.edac64bd4c371a8965825d092e64064b8be59917: This is why I am looking for an exceptional soul to be regent of this fair kingdom of Miscellania.
textByHash.993f432993bdf8786f85aec35a7e0690ccdd5032: [laugh][laugh]! You are bold, , but then I have yet to see a good ruler who was not.
textByHash.1513dd50b649770da30a84aa5ffae86fb57a9602: You will first have to demonstrate your worth. You are a good warrior, that I do not doubt; however ruling also requires a good grasp of diplomacy.
textByHash.715e2a5f5e93a5135dce8b2d359b2611acbeccc6: We have been at war with neighbouring Etceteria for hundreds of years; however, I think both of us have rather lost our appetite for hostilities.
textByHash.4a6a7e89dc4452118e4e782dc9d2b075b2f1bc22: Now may be an opportune time to make amends. If you manage to negotiate peace with them, then you will truly have shown your skill as a diplomat.
textByHash.6febb45467099cc18ae9f684d6a38e6d54753a0d: Wait! There is one more matter to discuss... a mere formality, of course.
textByHash.604d4c1fef5072d7cd40ca11dd3e8a3610b4a5b6: By Miscellanious tradition, one may not become regent of the kingdom unless in the trust of the royal family.
textByHash.fb08d00c9aae714d4b672658ee4ac6bcaeb6cd3d: This includes that of the future king or queen. It is yet too early for me to decide which of my children should inherit the throne.
textByHash.bae376e09cf002b195c0a879c0b24b5bd5e2b20a: Until then, gaining the trust of one of them should suffice. Who knows? Your choice might help me make a decision later.
textByHash.88591ef228c2eb971614f0d3f17cf4aa569784f7: So tell me! Which of my children will you attempt to gain the trust of?
textByHash.fd5a663ced9fe4e5f36add9a08bbab769f891f33: I am sure you can form a good working relationship with him. You can ask Ghrim for advice; that's what he's here for, after all.
textByHash.337d60e86fdfcd6762f1e2222f052573b0aa53f9: I am sure you can form a good working relationship with her. You can ask Ghrim for advice; that's what he's here for, after all.
textByHash.d955930924e50b13762bc1a47bfe5f8ac7c8b9f4: I can assure you that I managed to heed that same call, and ruling this land was no hindrance to me. However, I understand your decision. Fare thee well!
textByHash.0af8089adf93a4c55682c429610e7351bcd5f0c0: How are you getting on? Have you talked to Queen Sigrid of Etceteria?
textByHash.a4c84c21398abed8de920fa0369a5697a5807b7d: As you wish. I will keep this conversation between us.
textByHash.ea412918b556a57547e1c7facec30b5cc86688bf: Is that so? I can let them both know, but you may find that the Prince has taken the news rather coldly. A broken trust is difficult to mend.
textByHash.0f64ef661924e6852629b1ebe1e843c8adad62bd: Is that so? I can let them both know, but you may find that the Princess has taken the news rather coldly. A broken trust is difficult to mend.
textByHash.3f2c34d3bebf9ae9c30df5c60d20bce74b2f220f: Is that your honest opinion after having spent time with my son? The Prince might receive the news rather coldly. A broken trust is difficult to mend.
textByHash.3c0647853e24c83bb824652ca940f2596a0273d4: As you wish. I will pay heed to your recommendation.
textByHash.e62f56357834a29619b147b5867730e3ec0ebb79: Is that your honest opinion after having spent time with my daughter? The Princess might receive the news rather coldly. A broken trust is difficult to mend.
textByHash.918869439c3a9916f82cece0a9ec0893bd7e2664: Hmph! You can tell her I'll officially recognise her country as soon as she changes the Etceterian national anthem.
textByHash.01a78dea4988afe4720814aed467bd8c65f8a1eb: It's insulting! The title alone, "You're Stupid, We're the Best", should be enough to give you an idea!
textByHash.ac7e76d7c3fd11f98b5d312fc7ddc32c9c9bf2d5: How are you getting on? Have you convinced Sigrid yet?
textByHash.94fbbcdde018ca07f96b4c6897386cd9c9df7e9d: I doubt I will be able to hold an ordinary pen well enough to sign the treaty. You will have to find a pen large enough for me to hold.
textByHash.d199d7680a92c1596595a94d849b273acd620081: How are you getting on? Have you found me a pen yet?
textByHash.1c6d0bc37e11c6abc76d54598b944efc1257a27a: Excellent! And now, with a flourish, I sign the end of the rivalry between our two proud kingdoms!
textByHash.62d13d700a505ecab49e61f4bf8a3a6a9c9995a2: Now, how's it going with my son, will they accept you as regent?
textByHash.ef704ad8d4209e9adaa09a3ae047cb0bf4706e8f: Now, how's it going with my daughter, will they accept you as regent?
textByHash.c9547cd6e8dd9882ab0737ca462a4f00476d31f4: Ghrim tells me that the population is not yet very supportive of the idea of having you as ruler. You should try to gain their support.
textByHash.3ac26a1fae6a4b4ae05cbb813924212703a7e9e3: Ghrim tells me that the population would be glad to have you as a ruler.
textByHash.94de0cb695e52252198bef76a859225906ff2406: I see no reason to delay your regency any further. Ghrim, please proceed with the ceremony.
textByHash.5e101ab13fa583164d55d08eb13b92dae147855d: That is all. I wish you luck as Regent. Speak to Ghrim if you require assistance.
